summary: Draft PR for mn name/menu decomp work. It added several mnname.c function bodies, converted many mnname.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures, updated related gm and mnnamenew prototypes, and added an mnname include to mndiagram.c. Bot report showed 3 new matched functions and 9 improved unmatched mnname functions, but the PR had no body text, no human review comments, and was closed unmerged in the loaded metadata.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source file; replaced multiple /// stubs with C implementations and added typed access to persistent name data, name table traversal, page/column counts, JObj animation helpers, cleanup helpers, and forbidden-name checking.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnName_8023749C, CreateNameAtIndex, fn_80238540, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, mnName_802388D4, mnName_80238A04, mnName_80239EBC, mnName_80239F5C, mnName_80239FFC, mnName_8023A058, IsNameNotAllowed
bot_report: 3 new matches and 9 improvements in main/melee/mn/mnname

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for mnname functions; many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations replaced with typed return values and arguments.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CompareNameStrings, IsNameUnique, DeleteName, CreateNameAtIndex, mnName_MainInput, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_802388D4, IsNameNotAllowed prototype updates

key_files.role: Related game-mode prototype update for persistent name initialization.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: InitializePersistentNameData changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void InitializePersistentNameData(u8 slot)
raw_diff: mnNameNew_MainInput changed to void mnNameNew_MainInput(void)

key_files.role: Included mnname.h, likely to expose updated mnname declarations to this translation unit.

what_changed.text: Recovered several mnname.c stubs into C bodies, including three bot-reported full matches: IsNameNotAllowed, CreateNameAtIndex, and mnName_8023A058.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: implementations for IsNameNotAllowed, CreateNameAtIndex, mnName_8023A058

what_changed.text: Improved additional mnname functions without fully matching them according to the bot: mnName_80238A04, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, mnName_8023749C, mnName_80239EBC, mnName_80239F5C, mnName_802388D4, fn_80238540, and mnName_80239FFC.
Replaced raw persistent-name offset access in existing code with struct field access through GetPersistentNameData(...)->namedata and x1A1.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GetNameText changed from ((u8*) GetPersistentNameData(slot) + 0x198) to ->namedata
raw_diff: IsNameValid uses struct gmm_x2FF8_inner* and data->namedata[0]
raw_diff: CreateNameAtIndex writes namedata[0] and x1A1

what_changed.text: Updated many headers from unknown prototypes to typed declarations in mnname.h, gmmain_lib.h, and mnnamenew.h.

smart_moves.text: The PR connected recovered call signatures to headers instead of leaving all signature knowledge local to mnname.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmmain_lib.h and mnnamenew.h prototype updates

smart_moves.text: Existing persistent-name pointer arithmetic was improved to typed field access where a struct field was available.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GetNameText and IsNameValid use struct gmm_x2FF8_inner and namedata

smart_moves.text: Small dispatcher and count helpers were expressed with ordinary C control flow such as switch and for loops.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount for loops over 0x78 slots

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a function signature, update the owning header and related call-site headers in the same PR slice rather than keeping UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Persistent name data accesses became clearer and more reviewable when recovered as struct field accesses instead of byte-offset arithmetic.
Bot score reports can distinguish fully matched functions from partial improvements and should be recorded narrowly; this PR had three full mnname matches and nine partial improvements.

naming_conventions.text: The PR kept many address-style names for unrecovered semantics, such as mnName_8023749C and mnName_802388D4, while using clearer semantic names where already present or supported, such as GetNameText, IsNameValid, CreateNameAtIndex, and IsNameNotAllowed.
Temporary local names were role-oriented in several places, such as table, ptr, terminator, data, first_char, count, rem, jobj, and list.
New temporary structs used local offset-style field names when semantics were not proven, for example MnName88D4Data with x14/x18/x1C/x24/x30 and MnNameData with x30/x3C.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural loops were used for repeated slot scans and child traversal.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnName_GetPageCount for loop over i < 0x78
raw_diff: mnName_GetColumnCount for loop over i < 0x78
raw_diff: mnName_802388D4 for loop walking child->next

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A goto-labeled loop shape remained in mnName_8023749C, likely as a matching tactic, but the PR evidence does not include a verifier note explaining why an ordinary loop did not match.
evidence_refs: bot_report: mnName_8023749C improved to 95.68%, not matched

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Small local structs were introduced to type object data enough to access known offsets while preserving unknown fields as padding.
JObj translation setters were expressed through direct field writes plus HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub and copied assert behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnName_80239EBC writes jobj->translate.z and calls HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub
raw_diff: mnName_80239F5C writes jobj->translate.x and calls HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Matched code increased by +316 bytes (+0.01%). Three new matches were reported: IsNameNotAllowed, CreateNameAtIndex, and mnName_8023A058. Nine additional mnname functions improved but remained unmatched.
No human review comments or reviews were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: PR 2079 mnName_8023749C goto loop_cond natural loop
mnname.c HSD_JObj translate.x translate.z HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub assert cleanup

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for changed files
raw_pr_json: body null, draft true, merged_at null
notes: The raw diff and changed-file metadata are strong for what changed.
The bot report is strong evidence for the listed match/improvement scores, but no local build, objdiff, or checkdiff logs beyond the bot comment are included.
There is no PR body and no human review feedback, so intent, acceptance, and rationale for unusual shapes such as goto labels or raw __assert blocks are not established.
The PR was closed with merged_at null in the loaded metadata; no merge or acceptance should be inferred.
Some source forms visible in the diff, especially copied __assert patterns and a goto loop, may conflict with current standards unless later evidence justifies them.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should follow recovered bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2079 demonstrates updating mnname and related headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS after recovering function bodies and call signatures.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer typed persistent-name fields over byte offsets
accepted_candidate_records.text: In mnname.c, GetNameText and IsNameValid moved from raw offset/pointer arithmetic to GetPersistentNameData(...)->namedata, and CreateNameAtIndex used namedata and x1A1 fields.

source_update_candidates.title: mnname.c owns name menu and persistent-name helper work
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/mn/mnname.c contains name menu logic and persistent-name helpers such as GetNameText, IsNameValid, CreateNameAtIndex, IsNameNotAllowed, page/column counting, and JObj cleanup/animation helpers.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-level classification for future searches, but should be curated against current source before adding as a stable path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: Record verifier rationale for generated-looking goto loops
source_update_candidates.text: mnName_8023749C used explicit goto labels and remained only a partial improvement in the bot report. Current natural-loop standards already caution against preserving generated-looking blocks without evidence; curator may decide whether this PR should be indexed as an example of weak evidence rather than a new standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential negative example for standards/search, not an accepted tactic because the PR lacks verifier rationale.

source_update_candidates.title: Expanded JObj assert/setter bodies need cleanup evidence
source_update_candidates.text: mnName_80239EBC and mnName_80239F5C manually contain __assert blocks and JObj dirty-flag behavior. Current standards prefer mapping expanded header/assert behavior back to helpers/macros when evidence identifies them; curator may index this PR as a cautionary case if current source confirms cleanup was later needed.
source_update_candidates.reason: Visible source shape raises a standards concern, but the PR slice does not prove whether the shape was required for matching.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the PR was accepted or merged; loaded metadata says CLOSED and merged_at null.
Do not promote the goto loop in mnName_8023749C as an endorsed matching tactic; it was not fully matched and lacks a verifier explanation.
Do not promote the raw __assert/JObj setter expansion as preferred style; current standards suggest possible cleanup, and the PR slice lacks rationale.
Do not infer semantic names for address-style functions beyond the signatures and local behavior shown in the diff.
